title: Weatherization Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Utility Assistance > Weatherization Programs 38 Matching Service Providers ACCESS WEATHERIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to help reduce energy costs. Services may include insulation, heating duct repairs, air filtration, and other efficiency upgrades. Assistance is based on availability of funding and no emergency assistance is available. 3630 Aviation Way Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: Households must not exceed annual income guidelines for household size. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 779-6691 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER 50+ FIX-IT BRIGADE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides volunteers to assist with short-term and one-time assistance with household needs. Services include changing light bulbs and filters; seasonal yard work; installing railings, locks, grab bars; building risers for chairs, sofas, and beds; minor caulking and weather stripping; and other minor home repairs. Provides referrals to businesses that offer senior discounts. Does not offer repairs on small appliances, electrical, wheelchair ramps, or rentals. 2615 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 50 and older that would benefit from services Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-2pm Email (503) 588-6303 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY HOUSING AND COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT DIVISION WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization and energy education. Weatherization can include insulation, air sealing, and other measures designed to keep heat in the home during the winter and cool during the summer. The program is offered for single-family homes, mobile and manufactured homes, and in certain circumstances, multi-family unitis. Weatherization measures vary by home. 2051 Kaen Road Suite 245 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: -Household income must be at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level with priority given to older adults, people with disabilities, and families with children under the age of 18 -For applicants in multi-family dwelling (duplex, apartment, etc.), all people living in the building must apply Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm Email (503) 650-3338 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON MAIN OFFICE WEATHERIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to lower monthly utility bills and improve home energy efficiency. Weatherization includes insulation installation and air sealing. Work is performed by local contractors in accordance with state and local building codes and is then inspected by certified staff. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Income-eligible homeowners and renters (upon landlord's approval) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM COMMUNITY ACTION TEAM REGIONAL HOUSING CENTER WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides grant funding for measures that help reduce energy costs, such as insulation, furnace repairs, health and safety repairs, and refrigerator replacement. 125 N 17th Street Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Homeowners and renters with household incomes no higher than 60% of statewide median income Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 366-6589 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY BEAVERTON MULTI-SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY CONSERVATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to help households lower their utility bills. Services include in-home visits by an educator/auditor who provides energy-saving tips, efficient lighting, and water-saving devices. Households may also qualify for comprehensive weatherization services that include an energy audit visit to determine which energy-saving measures can be installed. Private contractors then install the recommended cost-effective measures, which may include ceiling, floor, or wall insulation; EPDM roofs (a durable synthetic rubber material); duct sealing; water heaters; heating systems; exhaust fans; or energy-efficient window and refrigerator replacements. 17933 NW Evergreen Place Suite 315 Portland, OR 97229 Eligibility: Homeowners and renters earning no more than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level: -1-person household: $25,520/year or $2,126/month gross -2-person household: $34,480/year or $2,873/month gross -3-person household: $43,440/year or $3,620.00/month gross -4-person household: $52,400/year or $4,366/month gross -5-person household: $61,360/year or $5,113/month gross Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 906-6550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY HILLSBORO MULTI-SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY CONSERVATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to help households lower their utility bills. Services include in-home visits by an educator/auditor who provides energy-saving tips, efficient lighting, and water-saving devices. Households may also qualify for comprehensive weatherization services that include an energy audit visit to determine which energy-saving measures can be installed. Private contractors then install the recommended cost-effective measures, which may include ceiling, floor, or wall insulation; EPDM roofs (a durable synthetic rubber material); duct sealing; water heaters; heating systems; exhaust fans; or energy-efficient window and refrigerator replacements. 1001 SW Baseline Street Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Homeowners and renters earning no more than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level: -1-person household: $25,520/year or $2,126/month gross -2-person household: $34,480/year or $2,873/month gross -3-person household: $43,440/year or $3,620.00/month gross -4-person household: $52,400/year or $4,366/month gross -5-person household: $61,360/year or $5,113/month gross Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 906-6550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION WASHINGTON COUNTY TIGARD MULTI-SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY CONSERVATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to help households lower their utility bills. Services include in-home visits by an educator/auditor who provides energy-saving tips, efficient lighting, and water-saving devices. Households may also qualify for comprehensive weatherization services that include an energy audit visit to determine which energy-saving measures can be installed. Private contractors then install the recommended cost-effective measures, which may include ceiling, floor, or wall insulation; EPDM roofs (a durable synthetic rubber material); duct sealing; water heaters; heating systems; exhaust fans; or energy-efficient window and refrigerator replacements. 11515 SW Durham Road Suite E8 Tigard, OR 97224 Eligibility: Homeowners and renters earning no more than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level: -1-person household: $25,520/year or $2,126/month gross -2-person household: $34,480/year or $2,873/month gross -3-person household: $43,440/year or $3,620.00/month gross -4-person household: $52,400/year or $4,366/month gross -5-person household: $61,360/year or $5,113/month gross Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 906-6550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC COMMUNITY CONNECTION OF NORTHEAST OREGON INC ADMINISTRATION OFFICE WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps reduce heat waste, improve indoor air quality, and address health/safety issues by caulking, insulating, doing duct work, and replacing doors. May also help replace exhaust fans and furnaces. 2802 Adams Avenue La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Households with income no higher than 200% of the Federal Poverty Level Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 963-3186 1006 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT WEATHERIZATION AND LEAD WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides workshops on basic weatherization, DIY insulation, and basic lead poisoning prevention. Qualified participants also receive a free kit of materials including items such as reusable vinyl storm window kits, door weather stripping, and pipe insulation. Materials are also available for purchase. Also offers in-home repairs and energy efficiency upgrades. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Workshops: Unrestricted, but free supplies are restricted to households earning no more than 80% median family income In-home weatherization and small safety repairs: Age 55 and older and adults with disabilities earning no more than 80% median family income Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:30am-5pm Workshops typically late September to early February, depending on weather and funding availability Email (503) 284-6827 108 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ENERGY PROJECT DIY SUMMER WEATHERIZATION WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Teaches low-cost cooling and energy saving techniques for the home. Includes tips for saving water and energy, cooling homes without using air conditioning, and preventing heat-related illness. Qualified participants receive a free kit of materials to lower energy costs and stay cooler in their home during the warmer months. 2705 E Burnside Street Suite 112 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Workshops: Unrestricted, but free supplies are restricted to households earning no more than 80% median family income Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 9:30am-5pm. Workshop dates vary. Email (503) 284-6827 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY IN ACTION WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home health and safety modifications designed to lower the cost of home heating. Modifications may include duct sealing, ceiling and wall insulation, fan replacement, window and door replacement, or addressing additional issues found in the home after inspection. 915 SW 3rd Avenue Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Household income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm Email (541) 889-9555 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY SERVICES CONSORTIUM WEATHERIZATION ANNEX WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists in weatherizing homes. The following assessments can be performed to identify energy-related problems in the home: heating system safety checks, diagnostic combustion tests, insulation in attics and walls, and duct sealing. 1981 Fescue Street SE Albany, OR 97322 Eligibility: Income 200% or below the federal poverty level Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 738-0958 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ENERGY TRUST OF OREGON ENERGY CONSERVATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides cash incentives and support for energy-saving and renewable energy projects in homes, industrial, and agricultural businesses. Incentives help pay for upgrades like insulation, duct sealing, heat pumps (which heat and cool homes), and efficient water heaters in single-family and multifamily homes. Some upgrades may also qualify for state and federal tax credits. Also offers free and low-cost tips to help save energy and reduce bills 920 SW Sixth Avenue Suite 900 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Varies by service. Serves Portland General Electric, Pacific Power, Cascade Natural Gas, and Avista customers in Oregon and NW Natural customers in Oregon and Washington. Services are available for homeowners, condo and townhome owners, and renters. Income guidelines vary by program. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 368-7878 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EUGENE WATER AND ELECTRIC BOARD INCOME-BASED EFFICIENCY REBATES AND LOANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides efficiency products through a rebate; loan program upgrades may include heating systems, insulation, water leak repair assistance, and windows. Income-based efficiency rebates and loans are for homes with existing electric heating and water heating. 4200 Roosevelt Boulevard Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Must meet income limits for household size. Total household income includes gross income for all adults over 18, including roommates. Visit website for current income limits. -Documented qualifying income level for the previous month is required. -Only the qualifying account holder's primary residence may qualify for owner-occupied homes. For rentals, the tenant must be income qualified and be the current EWEB account holder. -Maximum aggregated income-based rebates are $8,000 per customer and $8,000 per residence over 10 years. Each individual product may have a limit as well. -Applications may be denied for closed accounts, abusive behavior toward EWEB, tampering with any EWEB property, or any evidence of fraud. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 685-7088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HARNEY COUNTY HARNEY HUB SENIOR AND COMMUNITY CENTER SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides utility assistance for gas, electric, and heating fuel bills; motel vouchers; one-time gas vouchers; transportation passes; food pantry (visit twice per month); Fresh Alliance program (provides fresh produce, dairy, and bread products); weatherization services; and blood pressure screenings. Offers snack kits. Assistive medical equipment and mobility aids also available. 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: -Food pantry: Households at or below 300% federal poverty level. No proof of income is required. -Fresh Alliance: Unrestricted -Snack kits for those looking to keep moving through the community -Motel vouchers: Homeless individuals requiring one night's stay -Gas vouchers: Must be stuck and looking to continue to move through the community -Transportation passes: Must have no ties to the community and looking to continue to move through the community -Energy Assistance: Household must meet federal poverty guidelines Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-4:30pm Fresh Alliance: Monday 10am-10:30am Food pantry: Wednesday 10am-1pm, Thursday 4pm-6pm Email (541) 573-6024 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOMES FOR GOOD WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to income-eligible homeowners and renters. Older adults and adults with disabilities are prioritized. Job costs are paid by funding from federal grants and participating utilities. Energy audits and inspections are done by agency staff. Installation is completed by licensed and bonded private contractors. Weatherization measures designed to reduce energy consumption may include: -Attic, wall, and floor insulation -Improved venting in attic and underfloor -Whole house air sealing and installation of continuous ventilation -Weatherstripping of doors -Installation of thermal replacement windows -Installation of carbon monoxide detectors and smoke alarms -Sealing and insulation of HVAC ducts -Diagnostic testing to identify and locate air leakage -Servicing of furnaces and installation of digital thermostats -Minor weatherization-related health and safety repairs (no emergency home repairs) -Installation of energy-efficient refrigerators and compact fluorescent bulbs 100 W 13th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Income-qualified applicants (homeowners or renters). Certain households are given priority in accordance with federal guidelines, as well as available funding resources: 1st priority: Households with an older adult (age 60 or older) 2nd priority: Households with a member who receives Social Security Disability benefits 3rd priority: Households with children age 6 and under 4th priority: All other households Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 682-2561 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL MID COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL HOOD RIVER UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides energy payment assistance programs, emergency assistance for electric bills, heating fuel payment assistance, low-income water assistance programs, water, and garbage discount programs. 606 State Street Suite 1b Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: -Energy assistance: Low-income households -Emergency assistance: Low-income households with past due or shut-off notices -Water or power discounts: Residents served by the City of Hood River Water who have an active Pacific Power account in the applicant's name. Apartments or rentals that have water paid by another party are eligible for the discount as long as they are within the service area of City of Hood River. The City applies a discount to the client’s Pacific Power bill in lieu of a water bill discount in that case. -Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities: Monthly power, water, sewer, and garbage bill discounts for customers of City of The Dalles, Northern Wasco County PUD, and The Dalles or Hood River Disposal -Outreach: Must live within the service area and demonstrate crisis or a special need. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 298-5131 206 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER UTILITY ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides energy payment assistance programs, emergency assistance for electric bills, heating fuel payment assistance, low-income water assistance programs, water, and garbage discount programs. 2505 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: -Energy assistance: Low-income households -Emergency assistance: Low-income households with past due or shut-off notices -Water or power discounts: Residents served by the City of Hood River Water who have an active Pacific Power account in the applicant's name. Apartments or rentals that have water paid by another party are eligible for the discount as long as they are within the service area of City of Hood River. The City applies a discount to the client’s Pacific Power bill in lieu of a water bill discount in that case. -Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities: Monthly power, water, sewer, and garbage bill discounts for customers of City of The Dalles, Northern Wasco County PUD, and The Dalles or Hood River Disposal -Outreach: Must live within the service area and demonstrate crisis or a special need. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 298-5131 200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY WEATHERIZATION SERVICES WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization assistance to help lower the energy usage of low-income households. Services may include weather-stripping of doors and windows, floor, wall, and ceiling insulation, duct insulation and sealing. Also offers information on health and safety, as well as energy education. 4700 Silverton Road NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Household income must be at or below 200% of Federal Poverty Level (FPL). See website for income guidelines. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-5:30pm Email (503) 315-7055 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MULTNOMAH COUNTY DCHS YOUTH AND FAMILY SERVICES DIVISION WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Participants will receive an energy audit of the home. Weatherization measures may include insulation, furnace inspection, heating duct improvements, air sealing, ventilation, and energy education. 209 SW 4th Avenue Suite 200 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Low-income residents living in single-family homes, mobile homes, and apartments Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-7423 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT BEND OFFICE WEATHERIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides whole-home weatherization services, including a home audit that may include insulation installation, duct sealing, weather stripping, health and safety repairs, and replacement of inefficient appliances (e.g., furnaces, refrigerators). 20310 Empire Avenue Suite A100 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Income no higher than 200% of the federal poverty level. Applicants can be renters or homeowners and live in mobile homes or site-built homes. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 548-2380 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NEIGHBORIMPACT LA PINE OFFICE WEATHERIZATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides whole-home weatherization services, including a home audit that may include insulation installation, duct sealing, weather stripping, health and safety repairs, and replacement of inefficient appliances (e.g., furnaces, refrigerators). 51530 Huntington Road Suite 6 La Pine, OR 97739 Eligibility: Income no higher than 200% of the federal poverty level. Applicants can be renters or homeowners and live in mobile homes or site-built homes. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (541) 553-0497 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NW NATURAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides natural gas service. Offers flexible monthly payment plans, assistance options for customers with past-due accounts, and home improvements. Also responds to reports of natural gas leaks. Website lists agencies that may offer financial assistance to low-income customers. 250 SW Taylor Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Customer Service: Monday-Friday 7am-6pm Natural gas leaks: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 422-4012 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION COOS WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization assistance including: -Heating Source Repair: May be able to service, repair, or replace broken furnaces, heating systems, or wood stoves, or provide a permanent heat source in homes without an existing source. -Insulation: Provides increased insulation in site-built and mobile homes. Eligible areas include floors, attics, and walls in site-built homes and underbelly blown-in insulation in mobile homes. -Weatherization: Helps households save as much as $200 a year on electric bills. Provides up-to-date information on how to save energy and free energy-saving products. 1855 Thomas Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: 60% or below the median income for the State of Oregon Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 435-7750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION OREGON COAST COMMUNITY ACTION CURRY WEATHERIZATION ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization assistance including: -Heating Source Repair: May be able to service, repair, or replace broken furnaces, heating systems, or wood stoves, or provide a permanent heat source in homes without an existing source. -Insulation: Provides increased insulation in site-built and mobile homes. Eligible areas include floors, attics, and walls in site-built homes and underbelly blown-in insulation in mobile homes. -Weatherization: Helps households save as much as $200 a year on electric bills. Provides up-to-date information on how to save energy and free energy-saving products. 937 Chetco Avenue Suite D Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: 60% or below the median income for the State of Oregon Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 435-7750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HUMAN DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION REGION 30 WEATHERIZATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services to lower monthly utility bills and improve home energy efficiency. Weatherization includes insulation installation and air sealing. Work is performed by local contractors in accordance with state and local building codes and is then inspected by certified staff. 306 S 6th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Income-eligible homeowners and renters (upon landlord's approval) Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 883-7186 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REBUILDING TOGETHER PACIFIC NORTHWEST HOMEOWNER ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home repair services including carpentry, minor plumbing, painting, minor electrical work, debris removal, roofing, HVAC, weatherization, energy efficiency work, grab bars, and handrails. Nonprofit community facilities may also apply. Yard work is not offered as a primary service, but may be done if it is needed as a part of a larger project and there are volunteers able to do the work. Can not assist with rental properties, duplexes, recent purchases requiring repairs, or houses that are subject to foreclosure actions or actions to enforce liens. Eligibility: -Serves low-income households below 80% Area Median Income (AMI). -Applicants must own and live in their home and be financially and/or physically unable to complete repairs on their own. -Applicant mortgage is current or paid off and not at risk of default. -Repairs must be safety and/or livability repairs -Applicant intends to age in place. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 523-0738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REBUILDING TOGETHER PACIFIC NORTHWEST REBUILDING TOGETHER WASHINGTON COUNTY HOMEOWNER ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides home repair services including carpentry, minor plumbing, painting, minor electrical work, debris removal, roofing, HVAC, weatherization, energy efficiency work, grab bars, and handrails. Nonprofit community facilities may also apply. Yard work is not offered as a primary service, but may be done if it is needed as a part of a larger project and there are volunteers able to do the work. Can not assist with rental properties, duplexes, recent purchases requiring repairs, or houses that are subject to foreclosure actions or actions to enforce liens. 12550 SW 3rd Street Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: -Serves low-income households below 80% Area Median Income (AMI). -Applicants must own and live in their home and be financially and/or physically unable to complete repairs on their own. -Applicant mortgage is current or paid off and not at risk of default. -Repairs must be safety and/or livability repairs -Applicant intends to age in place. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (502) 523-0738 Get Directions Visit Website More Details REEDSPORT CHURCH OF GOD CHRISTMAS IN JULY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A volunteer program providing yard work, minor construction, and home repair for low-income residents. An example of minor construction would be a ramp for wheelchair-bound people. Volunteers will not do extensive roof work. 2191 Birch Avenue Reedsport, OR 97467 Eligibility: Low-income and older adult homeowners. Each home can use this program no more than two times. Hours: 2nd Saturday of July Email (541) 271-3928 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALEM ELECTRIC WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization measures to Salem Electric residential members including ceiling, floor, wall, and duct insulation; caulking and weather stripping; and air sealing improvements. Incentives for window replacements are also available. 633 7th Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: Salem Electric residential members (homeowners and renters) with permanently installed electric heat who meet income guidelines could qualify for free weatherization measures. Members with heating fuels other than electricity may qualify for other programs. Household gross income limited to 60% of Oregon's median income. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-5:30pm Email (503) 362-3601 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK GARDEN VALLEY SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services that focus on helping families lower their energy use and utility costs, improve indoor air quality, and provide a healthier, more comfortable home. Service may include insulating attics, walls, heating ducts, and floors; sealing air leaks; improving attic and under-floor air flow; installing carbon monoxide and smoke alarms; and providing air purifiers and air filters. May also include servicing heating equipment and performing minor health and safety repairs. 251 NE Garden Valley Boulevard Suite 111 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-3pm (541) 492-3902 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK UNITED COMMUNITY ACTION NETWORK JOSEPHINE COUNTY SERVICE CENTER WEATHERIZATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides weatherization services that focus on helping families lower their energy use and utility costs, improve indoor air quality, and provide a healthier, more comfortable home. Service may include insulating attics, walls, heating ducts, and floors; sealing air leaks; improving attic and under-floor air flow; installing carbon monoxide and smoke alarms; and providing air purifiers and air filters. May also include servicing heating equipment and performing minor health and safety repairs. 900 SE 8th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-3pm (541) 956-4064 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE RURAL DEVELOPMENT OREGON STATE HOME REPAIR ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The Section 504 Home Repair Loan and Grant Program helps homeowners in eligible rural areas remove health and safety hazards, repair, improve, or modernize their homes, and make them accessible to people with disabilities. The maximum loan amount is $40,000 (the term is 20 years at 1%), and the maximum grant amount is $10,000. Loans and grants can be combined for assistance. 1220 SW 3rd Avenue Suite 1801 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: -Live in a rural area (population of 35,000 or less) -Be the homeowner and occupy the house -Unable to obtain affordable credit elsewhere -Household income that does not exceed the limit by county -Grants: Age 62 and older and not be able to repay a repair loan -Manufactured homes in parks do not qualify for mortgage loans Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (866) 923-5626 2 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YAMHILL COMMUNITY ACTION PARTNERSHIP WEATHERIZATION AND ENERGY EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Weatherization services are available to help improve home safety, maximize energy efficiency, and promote housing affordability. Weatherization services may include reducing heat waste by reducing air infiltration in the following ways: -Replacing broken window panes, re-glazing windows, applying interior caulking, installing new weather-stripping on windows and doors, and insulating water pipes. -Efficiency Upgrades LED lighting, low-flow shower, and faucet kits. -Heating Systems: Pressure test the forced-air system, air seal, and insulate the supply and return duct system. -Install as appropriate: flame retention burners, electronic ignition devices, and heating system repairs. -Attic, wall, and floor insulation. -Skirting: Install appropriate materials around the perimeter of the dwelling unit to control airflow in the crawl space. Energy education services include one-on-one client meetings, energy audits conducted by an energy technician, and workshops. Low-cost self-help kits are available to households to reduce their energy burden. 1317 NE Dustin Court McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: -Homeowners or renters with household income must be at or below 200% of Federal Poverty -Renters: Duplex or a quadraplex, 50% of the units must be occupied by income-eligible households for the entire building to qualify for weatherization. In other multi-dwelling applications, it is required that 66% of the units meet the gross income guidelines.
